title: Climbing Stairs
description:
You are climbing a staircase that takes `n` steps to reach the top. Each time
you can climb either 1 or 2 steps. Return the number of distinct ways to reach
the top.

solutions.summary: Ways(n) = Ways(n-1) + Ways(n-2); roll two values forward.

steps.title: Start from the function shape
steps.explanation: Begin with the signature and a default `0` return.

steps.title: Handle small staircases
steps.explanation: For 1 or 2 steps the count is just `n`.

steps.title: Seed the last two counts
steps.explanation: `a` is the ways to reach step 1, `b` the ways to reach step 2.

steps.title: Loop up to n
steps.explanation: Advance the pair once per step from 3 to `n`.

steps.title: Roll the counts forward
steps.explanation: The ways to reach the next step is the sum of the previous two.

steps.title: Start from the function shape
steps.explanation: Begin with the signature and a default `0` return.

steps.title: Handle small staircases
steps.explanation: The base case stops the recursion: 1 or 2 steps give `n` ways.

steps.title: Recurse on the recurrence
steps.explanation: Ways to the top is ways from one step below plus two steps below.
